title: Deprecate/remove stride argument for `Delta` / `DoubleDelta` + forbid Delta on non-numeric types
description:
As per #84383, it is no longer allowed to combine codec `DoubleDelta` and data type `FixedString(N)`.
This fixes
```sql
CREATE TABLE t0 (c0 FixedString(9) CODEC(DoubleDelta(1))) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY tuple();
INSERT INTO TABLE t0 (c0) VALUES ('a'); -- threw an error
```
To be precise, we required `datatype.isValueUnambiguouslyRepresentedInFixedSizeContiguousMemoryRegion() = 1`, now we require `datatype.isValueRepresentedByNumber() = 1`. So this affects UUID and Array as well.
Thinking about it,
1) It would be logical to apply the same change also for codec `Delta` just for consistency, even if there are no known data corruption issues like for `DoubleDelta`. It seems generally weird to apply `Delta` or `DoubleDelta` to non-numeric types.
2) We should also phase out the user-provided codec argument (stride size) for `Delta` and `DoubleDelta`. Can't think of a use case for numeric types, including date/times, (let alone non-numeric types) where a stride size smaller than the type width makes sense.
Both 1) and 2) must be done under a new MergeTree setting such that old tables continue to load.
CC: @Ergus
bug_features_seed: SELECT * FROM t1 WHERE c0 IS NULL;
key_bug_features: Combining codec DoubleDelta with non-numeric data types
